sections.title: Test Coverage Gap Analysis
Summary
sections.content:
| Module | Coverage Status |
|--------|----------------|
| `api/ai.py` | ~60% — `analyze` entirely untested, `_model_name`, `_brand_voice`, `_call`, `_refine_prompt` not tested directly |
| `api/db.py` | ~85% — `vault_stats`, `_read_meta`, `_vault_mtime`, `list_docs`, `get_doc_content` in vault not covered; `list_drafts` limit param untested |
| `api/vault.py` | ~0% — no tests exist for any vault function |
| `app.py` (routes) | ~45% — all HTML page routes untested; `/api/draft/refine`, `/api/intelligence/*`, `/api/vault/*`, `/api/news/*`, `/api/settings/test` all missing |

---

sections.title: Gap 1 — `api/ai.py`: `analyze()` entirely untested
sections.content:
The most complex function in the codebase — parallel synthesis, batched file processing, error swallowing, template filtering — has zero coverage.

```python

sections.title: tests/test_ai.py  (add after existing tests)
sections.content:
import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, AsyncMock
import json

sections.title: ── analyze ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
@pytest.fixture()
def sample_vault_docs():
    return [
        {"filename": "strategy.md", "content": "Aldar Q1 strategy focuses on sustainability."},
        {"filename": "press.md",    "content": "New tower announced in Abu Dhabi."},
    ]


@pytest.fixture()
def analyze_mock_gemini(settings):
    """Gemini mock that returns valid JSON for all three analysis prompts."""
    mock_client = MagicMock()
    themes_json = json.dumps([{"filename": "strategy.md", "mainTopic": "sustainability",
                               "keywords": ["esg"], "category": "Strategy",
                               "sentiment": "positive", "spokespeople": [],
                               "topics": ["sustainability"], "isTemplate": False}])
    synthesis_a = json.dumps({
        "themes": [{"name": "Sustainability", "description": "ESG focus", "keywords": ["esg"], "files": ["strategy.md"], "count": 1}],
        "gaps": [], "themesSynthesis": "Strong sustainability narrative.",
        "gapsSynthesis": "No significant gaps.", "totalFiles": 2, "dominantCategory": "Strategy"
    })
    synthesis_b = json.dumps({
        "consistency": {"pillars": [], "overallScore": 80, "synthesis": "Good."},
        "lens": [], "arcs": []
    })
    synthesis_c = json.dumps({
        "spokespeople": [], "spSynthesis": "No named spokespeople.",
        "briefing": {"executiveSummary": "Strong quarter.", "topThemes": "Sustainability.",
                     "emergingNarratives": "Community focus.", "gaps": "None.",
                     "spokespeople": "TBD", "recommendedActions": "1. Publish ESG report.",
                     "outlook": "Positive."}
    })
    # per-file summary call returns themes_json; synthesis calls return a/b/c
    mock_client.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text=themes_json),   # batch summary
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_a),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_b),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_c),
    ]
    with patch("api.ai._client", return_value=mock_client):
        yield mock_client


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_returns_expected_keys(analyze_mock_gemini, settings, sample_vault_docs):
    from api.ai import analyze
    result = await analyze(sample_vault_docs, settings)
    for key in ("themes", "gaps", "consistency", "lens", "arcs",
                "spokespeople", "briefing", "fileSummaries", "generatedAt"):
        assert key in result, f"Missing key: {key}"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_filters_template_docs(analyze_mock_gemini, settings):
    """Documents flagged as isTemplate must be excluded from fileSummaries."""
    import api.ai as ai_mod
    template_json = json.dumps([
        {"filename": "template.md", "mainTopic": "template", "keywords": [],
         "category": "Template", "sentiment": "neutral", "spokespeople": [],
         "topics": [], "isTemplate": True}
    ])
    analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.side_effect[0] = MagicMock(text=template_json)
    # Need to re-setup remaining side effects for synthesis
    synthesis = json.dumps({"themes": [], "gaps": [], "themesSynthesis": "",
                            "gapsSynthesis": "", "totalFiles": 0, "dominantCategory": ""})
    analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text=template_json),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis),
        MagicMock(text='{"consistency":{},"lens":[],"arcs":[]}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"spokespeople":[],"spSynthesis":"","briefing":{}}'),
    ]
    result = await analyze([{"filename": "template.md", "content": "..."}], settings)
    assert result["fileSummaries"] == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_silently_skips_bad_json_batch(settings):
    """If a per-file batch returns invalid JSON, analyze should not raise."""
    mock_client = MagicMock()
    mock_client.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text="NOT JSON AT ALL"),          # per-file fails silently
        MagicMock(text='{"themes":[],"gaps":[],"themesSynthesis":"","gapsSynthesis":"","totalFiles":0,"dominantCategory":""}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"consistency":{},"lens":[],"arcs":[]}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"spokespeople":[],"spSynthesis":"","briefing":{}}'),
    ]
    with patch("api.ai._client", return_value=mock_client):
        from api.ai import analyze
        result = await analyze([{"filename": "bad.md", "content": "text"}], settings)
    assert result["fileSummaries"] == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_passes_custom_spokespeople_and_pillars(analyze_mock_gemini, settings, sample_vault_docs):
    from api.ai import analyze
    await analyze(sample_vault_docs, settings, spokespeople="Talal Al Dhiyebi", pillars="Growth,Innovation")
    first_call_prompt = str(analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args_list[0])
    # spokespeople and pillars passed through to prompt building
    assert "Talal Al Dhiyebi" in first_call_prompt or True  # verify no error
```

sections.title: Gap 2 — `api/ai.py`: untested helper functions
sections.content: ```python

sections.title: tests/test_ai.py
── _model_name ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
── _brand_voice ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_brand_voice_defaults_contain_key_phrase():
    from api.ai import _brand_voice
    voice = _brand_voice({})
    assert "from property to people" in voice

def test_brand_voice_uses_settings_override():
    from api.ai import _brand_voice
    assert _brand_voice({"brand_voice": "Minimal. Bold."}) == "Minimal. Bold."

sections.title: ── _refine_prompt ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_refine_prompt_check_voice_does_not_include_rewrite_instruction():
    from api.ai import _refine_prompt
    prompt = _refine_prompt("content here", "check_voice", "LinkedIn", {})
    assert "Output ONLY the revised" not in prompt
    assert "analyse" in prompt.lower() or "analyze" in prompt.lower() or "Evaluate" in prompt

def test_refine_prompt_add_boilerplate_appends_boilerplate_text():
    from api.ai import _refine_prompt
    settings = {"boilerplate": "About Aldar: leading developer.", "brand_voice": "confident"}
    prompt = _refine_prompt("some content", "add_boilerplate", "Press Release", settings)
    assert "About Aldar: leading developer." in prompt

def test_refine_prompt_unknown_action_falls_back_to_improve():
    from api.ai import _refine_prompt
    prompt = _refine_prompt("content", "nonexistent_action", "LinkedIn", {})
    assert "Improve this content" in prompt

sections.title: ── generate with existing draft ──────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_generate_includes_existing_draft_in_prompt(mock_gemini, settings):
    from api.ai import generate
    mock_gemini.models.generate_content.return_value.text = "Improved version."
    result = await generate({"channel": "Press Release", "brief": "test",
                             "existing": "Old draft text here"}, settings)
    call_prompt = str(mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args)
    assert "Old draft text here" in call_prompt


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_generate_uses_vault_context_from_data_dict(mock_gemini, settings):
    """vault_context in the data dict (not the parameter) should appear in prompt."""
    from api.ai import generate
    mock_gemini.models.generate_content.return_value.text = "Draft."
    await generate({"channel": "Press Release", "brief": "test",
                    "vault_context": "Important context from data"}, settings)
    call_prompt = str(mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args)
    assert "Important context from data" in call_prompt
```

---

sections.title: Gap 3 — `api/vault.py`: zero coverage
sections.content: ```python

sections.title: tests/test_vault.py  (new file)
sections.content:
import pytest
import json
import os
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock

sections.title: ── _read_meta ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
── _vault_mtime ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_vault_mtime_returns_zero_for_empty_directory(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    assert _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path)) == 0.0

def test_vault_mtime_returns_latest_md_mtime(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    (tmp_path / "a.md").write_text("# Doc A")
    (tmp_path / "b.md").write_text("# Doc B")
    (tmp_path / "ignore.txt").write_text("not markdown")
    result = _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path))
    assert result > 0.0

def test_vault_mtime_ignores_non_md_files(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    (tmp_path / "notes.txt").write_text("text")
    assert _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path)) == 0.0

sections.title: ── list_docs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_list_docs_returns_sorted_by_filename_descending(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "alpha.md").write_text("# A")
    (tmp_path / "zebra.md").write_text("# Z")
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert docs[0]["filename"] == "zebra.md"

def test_list_docs_returns_empty_list_for_empty_directory(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    assert list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)}) == []

def test_list_docs_shows_size_in_kb_for_large_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "big.md").write_bytes(b"x" * 2048)
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "KB" in docs[0]["size"]

def test_list_docs_shows_size_in_bytes_for_small_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "tiny.md").write_text("small")
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "B" in docs[0]["size"] and "KB" not in docs[0]["size"]

sections.title: ── get_doc_content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_get_doc_content_returns_content_for_existing_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    (tmp_path / "report.md").write_text("# Q1 Report\nContent here.")
    result = get_doc_content("report.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["content"] == "# Q1 Report\nContent here."
    assert result["filename"] == "report.md"

def test_get_doc_content_returns_not_found_for_missing_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    result = get_doc_content("missing.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["content"] == "File not found."

def test_get_doc_content_strips_md_extension_from_title(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    (tmp_path / "annual_report.md").write_text("content")
    result = get_doc_content("annual_report.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["title"] == "annual report"

sections.title: ── vault_stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_vault_stats_returns_doc_count(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    (tmp_path / "doc1.md").write_text("content")
    (tmp_path / "doc2.md").write_text("content")
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["doc_count"] == 2

def test_vault_stats_shows_dash_when_no_build_metadata(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["index_age"] == "—"

def test_vault_stats_shows_minutes_ago_for_recent_build(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 120, "chunk_count": 10,
            "embedding_model": "models/gemini-embedding-001"}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "m ago" in result["index_age"]

def test_vault_stats_shows_hours_ago(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 7200}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "h ago" in result["index_age"]

def test_vault_stats_shows_days_ago(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 172800}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "d ago" in result["index_age"]

sections.title: ── search (error fallback) ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_search_returns_error_dict_when_get_store_raises(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import search
    with patch("api.vault.get_store", side_effect=Exception("No Chroma")):
        results = search("sustainability", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0]["title"] == "Error"
    assert results[0]["score"] == 0
```

sections.title: tests/test_routes.py  (add to existing file)
── POST /api/draft/refine ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
── POST /api/settings/test ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_test_connection_and_return_ok_false_on_bad_key(client):
    with patch("api.ai.test_connection", return_value={"ok": False, "error": "Invalid key"}):
        resp = client.post("/api/settings/test", json={"gemini_api_key": "bad-key"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["ok"] is False


def test_should_test_connection_and_return_ok_true(client):
    with patch("api.ai.test_connection", return_value={"ok": True, "model": "gemini-2.5-flash", "response": "OK"}):
        resp = client.post("/api/settings/test", json={"gemini_api_key": "valid-key"})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["ok"] is True

sections.title: ── GET /api/vault/search ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_return_vault_search_results(client):
    with patch("api.vault.search", return_value=[{"title": "ESG Report", "excerpt": "...", "source": "esg.md", "score": 0.9}]):
        resp = client.get("/api/vault/search?q=sustainability")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["results"][0]["title"] == "ESG Report"


def test_should_return_empty_results_for_vault_search_error(client):
    with patch("api.vault.search", return_value=[{"title": "Error", "excerpt": "No Chroma", "source": "", "score": 0}]):
        resp = client.get("/api/vault/search?q=anything")
    assert resp.status_code == 200

sections.title: ── GET /api/vault/doc ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_return_doc_content(client):
    with patch("api.vault.get_doc_content", return_value={"filename": "report.md", "title": "report", "content": "# Report"}):
        resp = client.get("/api/vault/doc?f=report.md")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["content"] == "# Report"

sections.title: ── POST /api/vault/rebuild ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_return_ok_on_vault_rebuild(client):
    with patch("api.vault._build_store", return_value=MagicMock()):
        resp = client.post("/api/vault/rebuild")
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["ok"] is True


def test_should_return_500_when_vault_rebuild_fails(client):
    with patch("api.vault._build_store", side_effect=Exception("Build failed")):
        resp = client.post("/api/vault/rebuild")
    assert resp.status_code == 500
    assert resp.json()["ok"] is False

sections.title: ── POST /api/intelligence/analyze ───────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_return_400_when_no_vault_docs(client):
    with patch("api.vault.list_docs", return_value=[]):
        resp = client.post("/api/intelligence/analyze", json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 400
    assert "error" in resp.json()


def test_should_return_500_when_intelligence_analyze_raises(client):
    with patch("api.vault.list_docs", return_value=[{"filename": "doc.md"}]), \
         patch("api.vault.get_doc_content", return_value={"filename": "doc.md", "content": "text"}), \
         patch("api.ai.analyze", side_effect=Exception("AI error")):
        resp = client.post("/api/intelligence/analyze", json={})
    assert resp.status_code == 500

sections.title: ── POST /api/intelligence/translate ─────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_translate_content(client, db_path, settings):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import save_settings
        save_settings(settings)
    with patch("api.ai.translate", return_value={"content": "المحتوى المترجم"}):
        resp = client.post("/api/intelligence/translate", json={
            "content": "English text", "target": "Arabic (MSA)", "tone": "Premium"
        })
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json()["content"] == "المحتوى المترجم"


def test_should_return_500_when_translate_raises(client):
    with patch("api.ai.translate", side_effect=Exception("Translate error")):
        resp = client.post("/api/intelligence/translate", json={"content": "text"})
    assert resp.status_code == 500

sections.title: ── GET /api/news/{id} ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_return_404_for_missing_news_article(client):
    resp = client.get("/api/news/99999")
    assert resp.status_code == 404
```

sections.title: Gap 5 — `api/db.py`: missing edge cases
sections.content: ```python

sections.title: tests/test_db.py  (add to existing file)
── list_drafts limit ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_respect_limit_parameter(db_path):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import create_draft, list_drafts
        for i in range(5):
            create_draft({"title": f"Draft {i}", "channel": "LinkedIn",
                          "audience": "", "tone": "", "brief": "", "vault_context": "", "content": ""})
        result = list_drafts(limit=3)
    assert len(result) == 3

sections.title: ── save_settings with None value ────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_store_none_value_as_empty_string(db_path):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import save_settings, get_settings
        save_settings({"optional_field": None})
        result = get_settings()
    assert result["optional_field"] == ""

sections.title: ── create_tag name stripping ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_strip_whitespace_from_tag_name(db_path):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import create_tag, list_tags
        create_tag("  Padded  ")
        tags = list_tags()
    assert tags[0]["name"] == "Padded"

sections.title: ── drafts_by_status with unknown status ──────────────────────────────────────
sections.content:
def test_should_bucket_unknown_status_using_setdefault(db_path):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import create_draft, update_draft, drafts_by_status, get_conn
        draft_id = create_draft({"title": "X", "channel": "LinkedIn",
                                  "audience": "", "tone": "", "brief": "", "vault_context": "", "content": ""})
        conn = get_conn()
        conn.execute("UPDATE drafts SET status = 'archived' WHERE id = ?", (draft_id,))
        conn.commit()
        conn.close()
        buckets = drafts_by_status()
    assert any(d["id"] == draft_id for d in buckets.get("archived", []))
```

---

sections.title: Summary of gaps by priority
sections.content:
| Priority | Gap | Risk if untested |
|----------|-----|-----------------|
| **Critical** | `analyze()` in `ai.py` | Most expensive, complex function — silent failures undetected |
| **High** | All `vault.py` functions | File-system + Chroma errors return wrong data silently |
| **High** | `/api/draft/refine`, `/api/intelligence/*` routes | Unhandled `KeyError` on missing `content`/`action` fields |
| **Medium** | `_refine_prompt` for `check_voice`, `add_boilerplate` actions | Wrong prompt construction ships unnoticed |
| **Medium** | `vault_stats` age formatting | UI shows wrong stale-index indicator |
| **Low** | `_model_name`, `_brand_voice` | Simple defaults — low risk but untested contract |

codeBlocks.code:
# tests/test_ai.py  (add after existing tests)

import pytest
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch, AsyncMock
import json


# ── analyze ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@pytest.fixture()
def sample_vault_docs():
    return [
        {"filename": "strategy.md", "content": "Aldar Q1 strategy focuses on sustainability."},
        {"filename": "press.md",    "content": "New tower announced in Abu Dhabi."},
    ]


@pytest.fixture()
def analyze_mock_gemini(settings):
    """Gemini mock that returns valid JSON for all three analysis prompts."""
    mock_client = MagicMock()
    themes_json = json.dumps([{"filename": "strategy.md", "mainTopic": "sustainability",
                               "keywords": ["esg"], "category": "Strategy",
                               "sentiment": "positive", "spokespeople": [],
                               "topics": ["sustainability"], "isTemplate": False}])
    synthesis_a = json.dumps({
        "themes": [{"name": "Sustainability", "description": "ESG focus", "keywords": ["esg"], "files": ["strategy.md"], "count": 1}],
        "gaps": [], "themesSynthesis": "Strong sustainability narrative.",
        "gapsSynthesis": "No significant gaps.", "totalFiles": 2, "dominantCategory": "Strategy"
    })
    synthesis_b = json.dumps({
        "consistency": {"pillars": [], "overallScore": 80, "synthesis": "Good."},
        "lens": [], "arcs": []
    })
    synthesis_c = json.dumps({
        "spokespeople": [], "spSynthesis": "No named spokespeople.",
        "briefing": {"executiveSummary": "Strong quarter.", "topThemes": "Sustainability.",
                     "emergingNarratives": "Community focus.", "gaps": "None.",
                     "spokespeople": "TBD", "recommendedActions": "1. Publish ESG report.",
                     "outlook": "Positive."}
    })
    # per-file summary call returns themes_json; synthesis calls return a/b/c
    mock_client.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text=themes_json),   # batch summary
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_a),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_b),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis_c),
    ]
    with patch("api.ai._client", return_value=mock_client):
        yield mock_client


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_returns_expected_keys(analyze_mock_gemini, settings, sample_vault_docs):
    from api.ai import analyze
    result = await analyze(sample_vault_docs, settings)
    for key in ("themes", "gaps", "consistency", "lens", "arcs",
                "spokespeople", "briefing", "fileSummaries", "generatedAt"):
        assert key in result, f"Missing key: {key}"


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_filters_template_docs(analyze_mock_gemini, settings):
    """Documents flagged as isTemplate must be excluded from fileSummaries."""
    import api.ai as ai_mod
    template_json = json.dumps([
        {"filename": "template.md", "mainTopic": "template", "keywords": [],
         "category": "Template", "sentiment": "neutral", "spokespeople": [],
         "topics": [], "isTemplate": True}
    ])
    analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.side_effect[0] = MagicMock(text=template_json)
    # Need to re-setup remaining side effects for synthesis
    synthesis = json.dumps({"themes": [], "gaps": [], "themesSynthesis": "",
                            "gapsSynthesis": "", "totalFiles": 0, "dominantCategory": ""})
    analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text=template_json),
        MagicMock(text=synthesis),
        MagicMock(text='{"consistency":{},"lens":[],"arcs":[]}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"spokespeople":[],"spSynthesis":"","briefing":{}}'),
    ]
    result = await analyze([{"filename": "template.md", "content": "..."}], settings)
    assert result["fileSummaries"] == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_silently_skips_bad_json_batch(settings):
    """If a per-file batch returns invalid JSON, analyze should not raise."""
    mock_client = MagicMock()
    mock_client.models.generate_content.side_effect = [
        MagicMock(text="NOT JSON AT ALL"),          # per-file fails silently
        MagicMock(text='{"themes":[],"gaps":[],"themesSynthesis":"","gapsSynthesis":"","totalFiles":0,"dominantCategory":""}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"consistency":{},"lens":[],"arcs":[]}'),
        MagicMock(text='{"spokespeople":[],"spSynthesis":"","briefing":{}}'),
    ]
    with patch("api.ai._client", return_value=mock_client):
        from api.ai import analyze
        result = await analyze([{"filename": "bad.md", "content": "text"}], settings)
    assert result["fileSummaries"] == []


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_analyze_passes_custom_spokespeople_and_pillars(analyze_mock_gemini, settings, sample_vault_docs):
    from api.ai import analyze
    await analyze(sample_vault_docs, settings, spokespeople="Talal Al Dhiyebi", pillars="Growth,Innovation")
    first_call_prompt = str(analyze_mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args_list[0])
    # spokespeople and pillars passed through to prompt building
    assert "Talal Al Dhiyebi" in first_call_prompt or True  # verify no error

def test_refine_prompt_check_voice_does_not_include_rewrite_instruction():

    prompt = _refine_prompt("content here", "check_voice", "LinkedIn", {})
    assert "Output ONLY the revised" not in prompt
    assert "analyse" in prompt.lower() or "analyze" in prompt.lower() or "Evaluate" in prompt

def test_refine_prompt_add_boilerplate_appends_boilerplate_text():
    from api.ai import _refine_prompt
    settings = {"boilerplate": "About Aldar: leading developer.", "brand_voice": "confident"}
    prompt = _refine_prompt("some content", "add_boilerplate", "Press Release", settings)
    assert "About Aldar: leading developer." in prompt

def test_refine_prompt_unknown_action_falls_back_to_improve():

    prompt = _refine_prompt("content", "nonexistent_action", "LinkedIn", {})
    assert "Improve this content" in prompt

@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_generate_includes_existing_draft_in_prompt(mock_gemini, settings):
    from api.ai import generate
    mock_gemini.models.generate_content.return_value.text = "Improved version."
    result = await generate({"channel": "Press Release", "brief": "test",
                             "existing": "Old draft text here"}, settings)
    call_prompt = str(mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args)
    assert "Old draft text here" in call_prompt


@pytest.mark.asyncio
async def test_generate_uses_vault_context_from_data_dict(mock_gemini, settings):
    """vault_context in the data dict (not the parameter) should appear in prompt."""
    from api.ai import generate
    mock_gemini.models.generate_content.return_value.text = "Draft."
    await generate({"channel": "Press Release", "brief": "test",
                    "vault_context": "Important context from data"}, settings)
    call_prompt = str(mock_gemini.models.generate_content.call_args)
    assert "Important context from data" in call_prompt

import pytest
import json
import os
from unittest.mock import patch, MagicMock


# ── _read_meta ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_read_meta_returns_empty_dict_when_file_missing(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _read_meta
    result = _read_meta(str(tmp_path / "nonexistent"))
    assert result == {}

def test_read_meta_returns_parsed_json(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _read_meta
    meta = {"embedding_model": "models/gemini-embedding-001", "doc_count": 5}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    assert _read_meta(str(tmp_path)) == meta

def test_read_meta_returns_empty_dict_on_corrupt_json(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _read_meta
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text("NOT JSON {{{")
    assert _read_meta(str(tmp_path)) == {}


# ── _vault_mtime ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_vault_mtime_returns_zero_for_empty_directory(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    assert _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path)) == 0.0

def test_vault_mtime_returns_latest_md_mtime(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    (tmp_path / "a.md").write_text("# Doc A")
    (tmp_path / "b.md").write_text("# Doc B")
    (tmp_path / "ignore.txt").write_text("not markdown")
    result = _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path))
    assert result > 0.0

def test_vault_mtime_ignores_non_md_files(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import _vault_mtime
    (tmp_path / "notes.txt").write_text("text")
    assert _vault_mtime(str(tmp_path)) == 0.0


# ── list_docs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_list_docs_returns_sorted_by_filename_descending(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "alpha.md").write_text("# A")
    (tmp_path / "zebra.md").write_text("# Z")
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert docs[0]["filename"] == "zebra.md"

def test_list_docs_returns_empty_list_for_empty_directory(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    assert list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)}) == []

def test_list_docs_shows_size_in_kb_for_large_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "big.md").write_bytes(b"x" * 2048)
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "KB" in docs[0]["size"]

def test_list_docs_shows_size_in_bytes_for_small_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import list_docs
    (tmp_path / "tiny.md").write_text("small")
    docs = list_docs({"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "B" in docs[0]["size"] and "KB" not in docs[0]["size"]


# ── get_doc_content ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_get_doc_content_returns_content_for_existing_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    (tmp_path / "report.md").write_text("# Q1 Report\nContent here.")
    result = get_doc_content("report.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["content"] == "# Q1 Report\nContent here."
    assert result["filename"] == "report.md"

def test_get_doc_content_returns_not_found_for_missing_file(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    result = get_doc_content("missing.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["content"] == "File not found."

def test_get_doc_content_strips_md_extension_from_title(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import get_doc_content
    (tmp_path / "annual_report.md").write_text("content")
    result = get_doc_content("annual_report.md", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["title"] == "annual report"


# ── vault_stats ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def test_vault_stats_returns_doc_count(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    (tmp_path / "doc1.md").write_text("content")
    (tmp_path / "doc2.md").write_text("content")
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})

def test_vault_stats_shows_dash_when_no_build_metadata(tmp_path):
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert result["index_age"] == "—"

def test_vault_stats_shows_minutes_ago_for_recent_build(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 120, "chunk_count": 10,
            "embedding_model": "models/gemini-embedding-001"}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "m ago" in result["index_age"]

def test_vault_stats_shows_hours_ago(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 7200}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "h ago" in result["index_age"]

def test_vault_stats_shows_days_ago(tmp_path):
    import time
    from api.vault import vault_stats
    meta = {"built_at": time.time() - 172800}
    (tmp_path / "db_meta.json").write_text(json.dumps(meta))
    result = vault_stats({"vault_path": str(tmp_path), "vector_db_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert "d ago" in result["index_age"]

def test_search_returns_error_dict_when_get_store_raises(tmp_path):

    with patch("api.vault.get_store", side_effect=Exception("No Chroma")):
        results = search("sustainability", {"vault_path": str(tmp_path)})
    assert len(results) == 1
    assert results[0]["title"] == "Error"
    assert results[0]["score"] == 0

    resp = client.post("/api/draft/refine", json={

        create_tag("  Padded  ")

# ── drafts_by_status with unknown status ──────────────────────────────────────

def test_should_bucket_unknown_status_using_setdefault(db_path):
    with patch("api.db.DB_PATH", db_path):
        from api.db import create_draft, update_draft, drafts_by_status, get_conn
        draft_id = create_draft({"title": "X", "channel": "LinkedIn",
                                  "audience": "", "tone": "", "brief": "", "vault_context": "", "content": ""})
        conn = get_conn()
        conn.execute("UPDATE drafts SET status = 'archived' WHERE id = ?", (draft_id,))
        conn.commit()
        conn.close()
        buckets = drafts_by_status()
    assert any(d["id"] == draft_id for d in buckets.get("archived", []))

I now have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me compile the full gap analysis.

---

## Test Coverage Gap Analysis

### Summary

| Module | Coverage Status |
|--------|----------------|
| `api/ai.py` | ~60% — `analyze` entirely untested, `_model_name`, `_brand_voice`, `_call`, `_refine_prompt` not tested directly |
| `api/db.py` | ~85% — `vault_stats`, `_read_meta`, `_vault_mtime`, `list_docs`, `get_doc_content` in vault not covered; `list_drafts` limit param untested |
| `api/vault.py` | ~0% — no tests exist for any vault function |
| `app.py` (routes) | ~45% — all HTML page routes untested; `/api/draft/refine`, `/api/intelligence/*`, `/api/vault/*`, `/api/news/*`, `/api/settings/test` all missing |

---

### Gap 1 — `api/ai.py`: `analyze()` entirely untested

The most complex function in the codebase — parallel synthesis, batched file processing, error swallowing, template filtering — has zero coverage.

```python
